heaviest_rainfall.status: The largest amount of rain ever recorded is on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over the 144.8 hour event.

heaviest_rainrate.status: A torrential downpour occurred on 02 September 2025 when the rain reached a maximum intensity of 34.3mm per hour.  This happened during a 15.0 hour storm when 21.19mm of rain fell.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 23 on 07 September 2025, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: There are no active storms in the area.  The previous storm started on 02 January 2026 and lasted 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: You are currently experiencing a cold snap that started on 2026-01-01 and so far it has lasted for 6 day.  This year there has been 1 cold snap, the average number is 3.6.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 26.7°C, observed on 29 April 2025 when the temperature fell to 0.4°C but also managed to reach 27.1°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 0 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The most recent tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and this has only occurred 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (196°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

run.status: The largest wind run ever was on 21 October 2012 with a value of 1737 miles.

speed.status: The highest wind speed ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 5.6°C), outside temperature (down 5.5°C), and inside temperature (down 3.1°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in pressure (down 5 mbar).

The past week and month had observable variation in inside temperature (up 6.6°C) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 37.5m lower than yesterday, 47.0m lower than the past week, 42.6m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 845m to a maximum of 1717m, averaging 1161.2m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 1208.2m and 1203.8m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; cold temperatures are forecasted. Outside; bitterly cold temperatures are expected. Also, very little to no wind will be present. Possible light showers, partial cloudiness with some periods of sun, and overcast conditions will limit sunlight. Weather patterns to stay the same.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-2.3°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
